title: The Rise of Local LLMs: Hermes, Qwen, and the Developer Workflow
body:
Large Language Models (LLMs) are completely reshaping how we approach software development. We're moving away from simply relying on massive, closed-source models in the cloud, towards a new era of powerful, open-weights models that you can run locally.

Models like **Nous Hermes** and **Qwen** (often praised by the community) are proving that you don't need a massive data center to get impressive reasoning and coding capabilities. These models are heavily optimized, fine-tuned on high-quality synthetic data and instruction sets, making them incredibly sharp at specific developer tasks.

### The Shift in Dev Concepts
For developers, this means the concept of an "LLM" is changing from a "chat assistant" to a "programmable reasoning engine." We are integrating them directly into our pipelines:
- **Code Generation & Review:** Having an instance of Qwen-Coder or Hermes running locally in your IDE via tools like Continue.dev, giving you blazing fast, private autocompletion.
- **Agentic Workflows:** Using frameworks where smaller LLMs act as agents—one writes code, another runs tests, and a third reviews the output. 
- **Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) at the Edge:** Instead of sending proprietary codebases to an API, developers can embed and query their documentation locally using these smaller, capable models.

The barrier to entry is lowering. With tools like Ollama or LM Studio, spinning up a local Hermes instance takes minutes. The future of development isn't just AI-assisted; it's AI-integrated, right on your local machine.

title: Navigating the World of IT Governance and Compliance
body:
While I love writing code, I've also discovered a strong passion for IT Governance, Risk, and Compliance (IT GRC). Code is only as good as the policies and security standards that govern it.

My first deep dive into this was a project designing a COBIT 2019 framework for PT Pegadaian Bandung. I focused on prioritizing governance objectives, analyzing capability gaps, and creating audit-ready deliverables. This experience opened my eyes to the importance of standardized requirements and change acceptance criteria.

This passion led me to an IT GRC role at PT Perkom Indah Murni in Jakarta. Here, I managed IT policies and ensured our managed services complied with strict standards like ISO 27001 and ITIL. I was conducting risk identification, supporting audit processes, and finding ways to automate compliance reporting. 

It's fascinating to sit at the intersection of technology and business strategy. Ensuring that a company's infrastructure is not only functional but also secure and compliant is a challenge I thoroughly enjoy tackling.

title: Building Applications: My Journey as a Full-Stack Developer
body:
Building software that solves real problems is what I enjoy the most. During my time as a Software Engineering Intern at PT Informatika Media Pratama, I got my hands dirty with real-world applications. 

One of my favorite projects was **CariProject**, a freelance marketplace. I built the entire stack using Django and PostgreSQL, deployed it with Docker, and integrated Google Auth and the Midtrans payment gateway. It was a massive learning curve, but seeing the whole platform come together—from user monitoring to the admin dashboard—was incredibly rewarding.

I also spent a lot of time working on **Prenava**, a maternal and child health app built with Laravel, React, and Flutter. It had complex features like a pregnancy calculator, community threads, and even an integrated shop. And then there was **Hawa**, a real-time weather dashboard I built using FastAPI and Vite React. Adding WhatsApp integration for early weather warnings was a fun challenge that taught me a lot about real-time notifications and external APIs.

I love the process of taking an idea and turning it into a functioning, reliable system. Every project has taught me something new, whether it's about backend scalability, frontend responsiveness, or integrating complex third-party services.

title: Early Days: From IT Support to Web Development
body:
My journey into technology really started to take shape during my time at Telkom University, but it was my early professional experiences that truly cemented my path. 

In early 2024, I took on an IT Support role at Syaamil Group. It wasn't just about fixing computers; I was tasked with indexing 600 pages of the Quran using a web base for their Qursa Application. This project required a lot of attention to detail and gave me a profound appreciation for how technology can preserve and organize important information. 

Later on, around late 2025, I joined the Government Research and Development team in Bandung as a Web Developer. Here, I had the opportunity to design and develop the SatuDesk website from the ground up, tailored specifically to user needs. It was an incredible learning experience that pushed me to think about user experience and solid web architecture. These early steps laid the foundation for everything I would build next.
